title: Pacemaker follow-up documentation

red_flags.text: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.
red_flags.rationale: Alert review was checked for follow-up applicability; no alert, urgency, device event, or treatment is inferred.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording received alerts, priority assigned by a qualified clinic, review, communication, and emergency-system limitations without generating alert programming, urgency, diagnosis, or treatment.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic

exact_section.heading: Section 8 - alert definition, review, and communication

follow_up.text: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.
follow_up.rationale: The multisociety device-clinic statement supports documenting device-follow-up context, transmission or in-clinic data review, qualified sign-off, communication, and arranged follow-up without interpreting device findings.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting that a transmission was triaged and reviewed by qualified staff, signed off, communicated, and linked to an already decided follow-up action.

source.title: 2023 HRS/EHRA/APHRS/LAHRS expert consensus statement on practical management of the remote device clinic

source_limitations: Device model and indication, implant date, leads, battery, sensing, pacing, arrhythmia episodes, alerts, symptoms, wound, connectivity, report date and quality, formal interpretation, programming, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
